name: RESPIRATORY DISTRESS SYNDROME OF NEWBORN
definition: RDS = SURFACTANT DEFICIENCY DISORDER = HYALINE MEMBRANE DISEASE acute pulmonary disorder characterized by generalized atelectasis, intrapulmonary shunting,

demographics.Histo: uniformly collapsed alveoli + variable distension of alveolar ducts + terminal

dominant_finding: decreased lung expansion (counteracted by respirator therapy)
distribution: diffuse reticulogranular texture (coincides with onset of clinical signs, maximum severity at
discriminating_features: decreased lung expansion (counteracted by respirator therapy)
symmetric generalized consolidation of variable severity:
diffuse reticulogranular texture (coincides with onset of clinical signs, maximum severity at
evolving hazy opacities to clearing over several days

general: decreased lung expansion (counteracted by respirator therapy)
symmetric generalized consolidation of variable severity:
diffuse reticulogranular texture (coincides with onset of clinical signs, maximum severity at
evolving hazy opacities to clearing over several days
prominent air bronchograms (distension of compliant airways)
streaky / mottled lucencies radiating from hila without branching / tapering often outlining
large subpleural cysts without definable wall usually at diaphragmatic + mediastinal

clinical.Prognosis: spontaneous clearing within 7-10 days (mild course in untreated survivors); death
